steps.title: Locate the graph's high point
steps.content: The sentence asks for the highest edge density in 1986. The graph's peak is for Class I-IV, and that value is about 160 meters per hectare. The choice must name both the class and the approximate value.

steps.title: Check the data details
steps.content: Choice A uses the right class but gives a lower value. Choice C and Choice D give about 145 meters per hectare for other classes, so neither identifies the overall 1986 high point.

steps.title: Complete the assertion
steps.content: The remaining choice gives approximately 160 meters per hectare for Class I-IV, matching the high in the graph. The answer is B.
